testcase.name: test_end_slash
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_client.TestClient testMethod=test_end_slash> get_mock = <MagicMock name='get' id='4449769120'>
@patch('requests.Session.get', return_value=GoodResponse) def test_end_slash(self, get_mock): > get("test", pypi_server="https://mock.test.mock/test")
tests/test_client.py:35: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
package_name = 'test', pypi_server = 'https://mock.test.mock/test/'
def get(package_name, pypi_server="https://pypi.python.org/pypi/"): """ Construct a request to the PyPI server and returns an instance of :class:`yarg.package.Package`.
:param package_name: case sensitive name of the package on the PyPI server. :param pypi_server: (option) URL to the PyPI server.
>>> import yarg >>> package = yarg.get('yarg') <Package yarg> """ if not pypi_server.endswith("/"): pypi_server = pypi_server + "/"
buffer = BytesIO() curl = pycurl.Curl() curl.setopt(curl.URL, "{0}{1}/json".format(pypi_server, package_name)) curl.setopt(curl.WRITEDATA, buffer) > curl.perform() E pycurl.error: (6, 'Could not resolve host: mock.test.mock')
yarg/client.py:52: error

testcase.name: test_get
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_client.TestClient testMethod=test_get> get_mock = <MagicMock name='get' id='4449770464'>
@patch('requests.Session.get', return_value=BadResponse) def test_get(self, get_mock): # Python 2.6.... try: > get("test")
tests/test_client.py:27: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
package_name = 'test', pypi_server = 'https://pypi.python.org/pypi/'
def get(package_name, pypi_server="https://pypi.python.org/pypi/"): """ Construct a request to the PyPI server and returns an instance of :class:`yarg.package.Package`.
:param package_name: case sensitive name of the package on the PyPI server. :param pypi_server: (option) URL to the PyPI server.
>>> import yarg >>> package = yarg.get('yarg') <Package yarg> """ if not pypi_server.endswith("/"): pypi_server = pypi_server + "/"
buffer = BytesIO() curl = pycurl.Curl() curl.setopt(curl.URL, "{0}{1}/json".format(pypi_server, package_name)) curl.setopt(curl.WRITEDATA, buffer) curl.perform()
response_code = curl.getinfo(curl.RESPONSE_CODE) curl.close()
if response_code >= 300: > raise HTTPError(status_code=response_code, reason='HTTP Error') E yarg.exceptions.HTTPError: <HTTPError 301 HTTP Error>
yarg/client.py:58: HTTPError
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
self = <tests.test_client.TestClient testMethod=test_get> get_mock = <MagicMock name='get' id='4449770464'>
@patch('requests.Session.get', return_value=BadResponse) def test_get(self, get_mock): # Python 2.6.... try: get("test") except HTTPError as e: > self.assertEqual(300, e.status_code) E AssertionError: 300 != 301
tests/test_client.py:29: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_newest_package
testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_parse.TestParse testMethod=test_newest_package> get_mock = <MagicMock name='get' id='4443139984'>
@patch('requests.get', return_value=GoodNewestResponse) def test_newest_package(self, get_mock): > p = newest_packages() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_parse.py:100: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ yarg/parse.py:72: in newest_packages items = _get(pypi_server) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
pypi_server = 'https://pypi.python.org/pypi?%3Aaction=packages_rss'
def _get(pypi_server): """ Query the PyPI RSS feed and return a list of XML items. """ buffer = BytesIO() curl = pycurl.Curl() curl.setopt(curl.URL, pypi_server) curl.setopt(curl.WRITEDATA, buffer) curl.perform()
status_code = curl.getinfo(pycurl.RESPONSE_CODE) if status_code >= 300: > raise HTTPError(status_code=status_code, reason='HTTP Error: {}'.format(status_code)) E yarg.exceptions.HTTPError: <HTTPError 301 HTTP Error: 301>
yarg/parse.py:46: HTTPError
